str:Code.com:Description: An investor that makes direct investment in enterprises, having control or significant influence. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the status of the parent entity's residency is unknown. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment enterprises in direct investor, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Enterprises in which direct investment is made by the investor. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is not a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposits: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Deposits that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, Repo Loans (reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents)
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents, specifically those involved in repurchase agreements (repo loans).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Debt instruments, Other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future, including SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, and other accounts receivable/payable. Broad classification for sectors not fitting traditional classifications.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, Other (reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents)
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities related to reserve assets owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Broad classification for sectors not fitting traditional classifications.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, General government, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs): allocations, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. SDR allocations are reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and allocated to its member countries to supplement existing reserve assets. Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are international reserve assets that the IMF allocates to its members as a form of supplementary liquidity. These allocations are based on each member country's IMF quota and are intended to provide additional liquidity and support to the global financial system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Central bank, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund and allocated to its members to supplement existing reserve assets. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Investment fund shares/units, Money market funds
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Shares or units in a fund that pools investor money to purchase securities. Investment funds that invest in short-term debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, credit, and loans with the IMF (other than reserves), Central bank, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments including loans, credit, and loans with the International Monetary Fund (IMF), excluding those held as reserves. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises that are both controlled by the same parent entity. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Other short-term foreign currency liabilities to nonresidents: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term foreign currency liabilities to nonresidents that are classified as reserve-related liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investor in direct invesment enterprises, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: An investor that makes direct investment in enterprises, having control or significant influence. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve Assets included in SDR basket
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve Assets included in SDR basket

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Investment fund shares/units
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Shares or units in a fund that pools investor money to purchase securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment enterprises in direct investor, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Enterprises in which direct investment is made by the investor. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Debt instruments, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future, including SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, and other accounts receivable/payable. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, credit, and loans with the IMF (other than reserves), General government, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments including loans, credit, and loans with the International Monetary Fund (IMF), excluding those held as reserves. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the status of the parent entity's residency is unknown. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is not a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit and loans from the IMF: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from credit and loans provided by the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions (net): nonproduced assets
str:Code.com:Description: The purchases of nonproduced assets, such as land and natural resources, during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Primary income
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Primary incomes are incomes earned by institutional units as a consequence of their involvement in processes of production or ownership of assets that may be needed for purposes of production.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial account balance (assets less liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: The financial account balance reflects the difference in transactions in financial assets and liabilities in the accounting period. In the external accounts, only transactions between residents and non-residents are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents total
str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, inter-company lending
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents arising from inter-company lending.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents: inter-company lending
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from inter-company lending.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, inter-company lending, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents arising from inter-company lending. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, General government
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents: inter-company lending, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from inter-company lending. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other sectors: Other
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other sectors: Other
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other sectors: Other, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Central bank, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, General government, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other sectors: Other, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, General government, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other financial corporations, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Central bank, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other sectors: Other
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other financial corporations, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents total, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Accounts that are receivable but not classified under specific categories like trade receivables or loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other equity
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Equity holdings that do not fall under the main categories of shares or stocks.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts payable
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Accounts that are payable but not classified under specific categories like trade payables or loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), Not sectorised
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund and allocated to its members to supplement existing reserve assets. Refers to entities or data that have not been assigned to any sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts receivable, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Accounts that are receivable but not classified under specific categories like trade receivables or loans. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Monetary gold
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Gold held as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs. Central bank

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts receivable, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Accounts that are receivable but not classified under specific categories like trade receivables or loans. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity and investment fund shares, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options, Monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options. Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes.

Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Rescheduling of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Rescheduling of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing) Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve position in the IMF, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Accumulation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank

str:Code.com:Name: Net International Investment Position, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: A financial statement that shows the value of a country's external financial assets and liabilities at a specific point in time. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Credit and loans with the IMF: other than reserves, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Credit and loans provided by the IMF that are not related to reserve assets. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Debt securities, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Credit and loans with the IMF: other than reserves, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Credit and loans provided by the IMF that are not related to reserve assets. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Debt securities, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total sum of all overdue payments including principal, interest, and penalty interest, specifically highlighted when part of unique financing solutions. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Non financial corporations, households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total sum of all overdue payments including principal, interest, and penalty interest, specifically highlighted when part of unique financing solutions. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Credit and loans with the IMF: other than reserves, Monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Credit and loans provided by the IMF that are not related to reserve assets. Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on penalty interest (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The accumulation of overdue payments specifically on penalty interest charges, distinguished when associated with exceptional financing conditions. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total amount of principal payments that have fallen due but remain unpaid, separately recorded in the context of unusual financial arrangements. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on original interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The build-up of overdue payments on the initially agreed interest or coupon payments, noted separately when related to extraordinary financing measures. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Non financial corporations, households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total amount of principal payments that have fallen due but remain unpaid, separately recorded in the context of unusual financial arrangements. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on penalty interest (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Non financial corporations, households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The accumulation of overdue payments specifically on penalty interest charges, distinguished when associated with exceptional financing conditions. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Securities under repo for cash collateral
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Securities under repo for cash collateral

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Accumulation of arrears on original interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Monetary authorities

Includes all non-financial sectors excluding households.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position
str:Code.com:Description: A financial statement that shows the value of a country's external financial assets or liabilities at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on original interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Debt instruments other than insurance, pension, and standardised schemes, Investment income, interest income

Debt instruments other than insurance, pension, and standardised schemes. The cost of borrowing money, expressed as a percentage of the amount borrowed, paid by the borrower to the lender for the use of the lender's money.

str:Code.com:Name: Current and capital account balance (credit less debit)

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Monetary gold: gold bullion
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Gold in the form of bars or ingots held as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Unallocated gold accounts
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Unallocated gold accounts.

Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions, claims on monetary authorities. Central bank

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs)

str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other sectors: Other
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, General government
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents total, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, General government
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other sectors: Other
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Currency and deposits, Claims on monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions, claims on monetary authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Currency and deposits, Claims on other entities
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions, claims on other entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs.

str:Code.com:Name: Total liabilities, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of all financial obligations owed by an entity to other entities. These obligations represent claims held by others against the entity and may include loans, debt securities, accounts payable, and other forms of debt. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Total assets, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Total assets This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6), Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6), Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Reseve Assets, Not in SDR basket
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve Assets, not included in the basket of currencies used to value Special Drawing Rights (SDRs).

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.
